commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

commit.message:
[#10181] feat(spark-connector): Support TableWritePrivilege for Spark 3.5+ authorization (#10194)

### What changes were proposed in this pull request?

This PR adds support for Spark 3.5's TableWritePrivilege interface to
enforce MODIFY_TABLE privilege when performing write operations (INSERT,
UPDATE, DELETE). Changes:
- Add loadTable(Identifier, Set<TableWritePrivilege>) method to Spark
3.5+ catalogs (Hive, Iceberg, JDBC, Paimon)
- Add loadTableForWriting and loadGravitinoTableForWriting helper
methods in BaseCatalog
- Upgrade Spark 3.5 version from 3.5.1 to 3.5.3
- Add integration test to verify authorization behavior In Spark 3.5+,
when a user without MODIFY_TABLE privilege attempts to INSERT, a
ForbiddenException will be thrown. In Spark 3.3/3.4, the same operation
succeeds because those versions don't support TableWritePrivilege.

### Why are the changes needed?

Fix: #10181

### Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?

No

### How was this patch tested?

Added tests.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(hypervisor): add arcbox-vm Firecracker sandbox library (#11)

* feat: initial implementation of firecracker-vmm

Production-grade Firecracker microVM manager with gRPC interface
compatible with arcbox-protocol.

- vmm-core: VmmManager, VmInstance, VmStore, NetworkManager, SnapshotCatalog
- vmm-grpc: MachineService, SystemService, VmmService (pause/resume/snapshot)
- vmm-daemon: daemon binary with TOML config, signal handling
- vmm-cli: management CLI with full VM lifecycle commands
- Transport: Unix domain socket + optional TCP
- Network: TAP interface lifecycle, IP pool (172.20.0.0/16), bridge attach
- Snapshots: full and diff, catalog with meta.json persistence

* feat: expose full fc-sdk parameters via VmSpec and gRPC

- config: add TokenBucketSpec, RateLimitSpec, IoEngine, CacheType,
  CpuTemplateSpec, HugePagesSpec, DriveSpec, BalloonSpec, VsockSpec,
  MemoryHotplugSpec, MmdsSpec, JailerConfig; extend VmSpec with 19
  new fields; extend FirecrackerConfig with process-level options
- manager: apply all new VmSpec fields in create_vm, add jailer mode
  support via JailerProcessBuilder, add update_drive,
  update_network_interface, update_balloon_stats_interval, flush_metrics
- proto: add CreateVm RPC with full VmSpec mapping; add UpdateDrive,
  UpdateNetworkInterface, UpdateBalloonStatsInterval, FlushMetrics RPCs;
  add shared message types (TokenBucket, RateLimiter, DriveConfig, etc.)
- grpc: implement 5 new VmmService RPCs with proto↔core type conversions
- fix: suppress clippy::result_large_err (fc_sdk::Error is 144B, external)
- fix: collapse nested if-let, fix print_literal warnings
- docs: update README with new RPCs, config schema, breaking change note

* refactor(grpc): remove VmmService, align gRPC layer with arcbox MachineService only

Drop the vmm.v1.VmmService extension (vmm.proto, vmm_svc.rs) and the
associated build step. The server now exposes only MachineService and
SystemService, keeping the interface strictly compatible with arcbox-protocol.
Update README accordingly.

* refactor: replace arcbox MachineService/SystemService with self-contained sandbox.v1

- Add SandboxManager in vmm-core with async boot, event broadcasting and TTL support
- Add SandboxService and SandboxSnapshotService backed by sandbox.v1 proto
- Remove dependency on external arcbox-protocol proto files
- Rewrite vmm-cli for sandbox API (create/stop/remove/list/inspect/events/snapshot)
- Update README to reflect new sandbox-centric architecture

* feat(sandbox): implement exec/run via vsock guest agent

Add host-side vsock client (vmm-core/src/vsock.rs) and in-VM guest
agent (vmm-guest-agent) to support command execution inside sandboxes.

- vmm-core/src/vsock.rs: binary frame protocol (MSG_START/STDIN/RESIZE/
  EOF / STDOUT/STDERR/EXIT), connect_to_agent() handshake, run() and
  exec() async APIs returning mpsc channels
- vmm-core/src/sandbox.rs: attach Vsock device (guest_cid=3) on boot,
  run_in_sandbox() / exec_in_sandbox() methods, state Ready→Running→Ready
  transitions on workload start/exit, idle event broadcast
- vmm-grpc/src/sandbox_svc.rs: wire Run and Exec RPCs to real vsock calls
- vmm-guest-agent: new Linux-only binary that listens on vsock port 52,
  dispatches piped (tty=false) and PTY (tty=true) execution, forwards
  stdout/stderr/exit frames back to host
- vmm-core/src/error.rs: add Vsock(String) error variant

* feat(sandbox): add jailer mode support with snapshot/restore and fix TAP lifecycle

- Implement full jailer mode for sandbox create, checkpoint, and restore:
  - Stage kernel/rootfs into chroot before boot; use chroot-relative API paths
  - Checkpoint: create snapshot dir inside chroot, chown to jailer uid/gid,
    pass chroot-relative paths to FC, then move files to catalog after pause/resume
  - Restore: copy vmstate/mem into new chroot, re-stage kernel/rootfs from
    SnapshotMeta, pass chroot-relative paths to LoadSnapshotParams
  - Store kernel_path/rootfs_path in SnapshotMeta to enable re-staging on restore
- Fix TAP interface lifecycle: use absolute /usr/sbin/ip path (daemon launches
  without /usr/sbin in PATH); delete stale TAP before create to handle crashes
- Fix remove_sandbox_impl to delete sandbox working directory, preventing stale
  vsock sockets that caused EADDRINUSE on subsequent creates/restores
- Implement Run/Exec vsock guest agent protocol in vmm-guest-agent with
  stdin passthrough, stdout/stderr streaming, and TTY resize support
- Update README with detailed Jailer Mode Internals (Create/Checkpoint/Restore flows)

* docs(readme): remove daemon/cli, add examples section

- Drop vmm-daemon and vmm-cli from crates table and architecture diagram
- Remove Quick Start CLI command section
- Remove [grpc] config block (no standalone daemon)
- Update Jailer internals flow diagrams to use SandboxManager API names
- Add Usage section with direct API and embedded gRPC code snippets
- Add Examples section describing sandbox_lifecycle and serve examples
- Update cross-compile output to vmm-guest-agent only

* refactor: remove vmm-daemon and vmm-cli, add examples

- Remove vmm-daemon binary crate (daemon entrypoint)
- Remove vmm-cli binary crate (management CLI)
- Add vmm-core/examples/sandbox_lifecycle.rs: full create→inspect→checkpoint→restore flow
- Add vmm-grpc/examples/serve.rs: embedded tonic gRPC server reference
- Add .cargo/config.toml for musl cross-compilation target
- Update workspace Cargo.toml to drop daemon/cli members
- Update vmm-core and vmm-grpc Cargo.toml dependencies accordingly

* feat(protocol): add sandbox.v1 proto to arcbox-protocol

Move sandbox.proto from vmm-grpc into the shared arcbox-protocol proto
directory so the definition is accessible across workspaces.  Update
build.rs to compile it (separate sandbox.v1 package, not merged into
arcbox.v1 due to conflicting type names).  Expose the generated module
as arcbox_protocol::sandbox_v1.

* refactor(arcbox-vm): merge vmm-grpc and vmm-guest-agent into vmm-core

Consolidate the three-crate workspace into a single crate:

- vmm-grpc → vmm-core/src/grpc/{mod,sandbox_svc,server}.rs
  Proto types exposed via vmm_core::proto::sandbox (tonic_build in the
  new build.rs).  SandboxServiceImpl, SandboxSnapshotServiceImpl, and
  serve() re-exported from the crate root.

- vmm-guest-agent → vmm-core/src/bin/vmm-guest-agent.rs
  Added process/term/signal nix features required by the binary.

- vmm-grpc/examples/serve.rs → vmm-core/examples/serve.rs
  Updated imports to vmm_core:: paths.

- Removed .gitignore and LICENSE files (inherited from root repo).
- Updated README to reflect the single-crate structure.

* Update hypervisor/arcbox-vm/Cargo.toml

* Update hypervisor/arcbox-vm/README.md

* Update hypervisor/arcbox-vm/README.md

* Update hypervisor/arcbox-vm/Cargo.toml

* Update hypervisor/arcbox-vm/vmm-core/src/bin/vmm-guest-agent.rs

* refactor(arcbox-vm): integrate as workspace member, flatten vmm-core

- Remove the standalone sub-workspace (hypervisor/arcbox-vm/Cargo.toml
  workspace def + Cargo.lock) and its inner vmm-core crate
- Move vmm-core/src, vmm-core/build.rs, vmm-core/examples up one level;
  rename crate from vmm-core to arcbox-vm
- Fix build.rs proto path depth (../../ instead of ../../../)
- Merge hypervisor/arcbox-vm/.cargo/config.toml into workspace root
  (x86_64-unknown-linux-musl linker)
- Add arcbox-vm and fc-sdk = "0.2.1" to workspace dependencies
- Switch resolver to "3" (edition 2024 default)
- Extend nix workspace features: +term +signal +user

* refactor: rename hypervisor/ to virt/

Rename the top-level directory to better reflect that it contains
general virtualisation crates (Virtualization.framework, KVM, VMM,
VirtIO, Firecracker) rather than a hypervisor-only layer.

- Move hypervisor/{arcbox-vz,arcbox-hypervisor,arcbox-vmm,arcbox-virtio}
  → virt/{...}
- Update all workspace member paths and dependency paths in Cargo.toml
- Switch pro/arcbox-snapshot to workspace dep for arcbox-hypervisor
- Update references in CLAUDE.md, README.md, copilot-instructions.md

* style(arcbox-vm): apply cargo fmt

* fix(arcbox-vm): roll back IP allocation when TAP creation fails

* fix(arcbox-vm): use VmState type for VmRecord.state instead of Debug string

* fix(arcbox-vm): validate tty dimensions fit u16 before casting

* refactor(arcbox-vm): remove unimplemented SandboxNetworkSpec fields

* fix(arcbox-vm): fix next_ip overflow, underflow, and host-bit masking

- Validate prefix_len is in 1..=30 in NetworkManager::new() to prevent
  shift overflow (prefix=0/32) and host_max underflow (prefix=32).
- Mask base address to network address before arithmetic so host bits in
  the CIDR input cannot corrupt candidate IPs.
- Replace `base | offset` with `network_base + offset`; addition is
  correct for sequential allocation within a contiguous subnet.
- Add tests: invalid prefix rejection, /30 exhaustion boundary.

* style(arcbox-vm): cargo fmt

* fix(vmm): use kernel 6.1.102 and retry vsock agent connection

Firecracker 1.10.x registers virtio devices via both ACPI and the
kernel command-line virtio_mmio.device= params.  On the 5.10.223
kernel the ACPI path claims the MMIO regions first, leaving the
command-line registrations with EBUSY.  The block device survives via
ACPI enumeration but the vsock virtio device never initialises, so
vm-agent cannot bind AF_VSOCK port 52 and the e2e vsock tests fail
with "connection closed".

Switch both CI workflows to vmlinux-6.1.102, which is the kernel
Firecracker v1.10 officially supports and which handles the ACPI +
virtio-mmio coexistence correctly.

Additionally, the "ready" event fires as soon as Firecracker confirms
the VM is running (not when the guest finishes booting), so
connect_to_agent could race against vm-agent startup.  Fix this by
splitting connect_to_agent into a retry loop and a single-attempt
try_vsock_handshake: "connection closed" responses (Firecracker's
proxy signal that no guest listener exists yet) are retried every
200ms for up to 30s; all other errors propagate immediately.

commit.message:
ci: add path filters to non-core workflows (#3306)

## What changed
Added path filters to 3 workflows that were triggering on every push/PR
regardless of files changed:

- **guards.yml**: Now filters to \.github/**\, \scripts/**\,
\crates/**\, \Cargo.*\, \ ust-toolchain.toml\
- **feature-matrix.yml**: Now filters to \crates/**\, \Cargo.*\, \
ust-toolchain.toml\
- **property-tests.yml**: Now filters to \crates/**\, \Cargo.*\, \
ust-toolchain.toml\, \ uzz/**\

Schedule and workflow_dispatch triggers are preserved.

## Why
These workflows were consuming GitHub Actions runner capacity on every
push (including docs-only changes), blocking CI Core (the merge gate)
from getting runners promptly. We observed 60+ non-core workflows queued
competing with CI Core for runners.

## What was tested
- YAML syntax is valid (follows existing ci-core.yml pattern)
- CI will validate the workflows parse correctly
- No behavioral change for Rust source changes (same triggers, just
filtered)

## Risks
- If we add a new top-level directory with Rust code, we'd need to add
it to the path filters. Low risk since the repo structure is stable.

## Docs touched
None

## Triage: A (MERGE NOW)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add 148 CLI and UI integration tests

Add two new integration test files:

- crates/cli/tests/cli_hardening_tests.rs (74 tests)
  Covers subcommand parsing, help text, env var config, output formatting,
  error messages, exit codes, version display, shell completions, profile
  file handling, verbose flags, global flag ordering, and argument validation
  for telemetry/safety/diag/plugin subcommands.

- crates/ui/tests/ui_hardening_tests.rs (74 tests)
  Covers consent dialog state machine, combo instructions rendering,
  safety banner behavior, warning level styling/theming, error sanitisation,
  AppState/command data types, consent flow serialisation, and edge cases
  for extreme values and empty fields.

Both files follow project conventions:
- No unwrap()/expect() in tests
- Result-returning test functions
- Consistent use of type TestResult

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
